question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a take() at a TTM exactly equal to a breakpoint boundary (e.g., block.timestamp == market.maturity - 1 days, - 7 days, - 30 days, - 90 days, - 180 days, or - 360 days), cause the piecewise-linear interpolation in settlementFee() to select the wrong interval (off-by-one in the strict-less-than comparisons), resulting in a settlement fee that is systematically lower or higher than the intended breakpoint value, causing overclaim or underclaim of claimableSettlementFee[loanToken]? Preconditions: market created with non-zero settlementFeeCbp at all breakpoints; taker executes take() with block.timestamp == market.maturity - N days for each N in {1,7,30,90,180,360}. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(offer at TTM == breakpoint) -> assert settlementFee(id, TTM) == expected breakpoint fee. Invariant tested: settlement fee must equal the exact breakpoint value when TTM equals a breakpoint, not the interpolated value of the adjacent interval. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed by up to the difference between adjacent breakpoints. Proof idea: unit test each boundary with TTM == breakpoint and TTM == breakpoint - 1 and assert fee continuity and correct interval selection.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing take() post-maturity (block.timestamp > market.maturity), cause the settlement fee to be computed using the 0d breakpoint (settlementFeeCbp0) rather than zero, resulting in a non-zero fee being charged on post-maturity trades that are intended to facilitate unwinding, effectively making post-maturity unwinding more expensive and potentially unclaimable if the fee is charged but no loan tokens are transferred? Preconditions: market past maturity; settlementFeeCbp0 > 0; taker executes take() with TTM = 0. Call sequence: warp(market.maturity + 1) -> take(offer) -> assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] increased by buyerAssets - sellerAssets. Invariant tested: post-maturity settlement fee must be the 0d breakpoint value, and the fee must be correctly deducted from the buyer's payment. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed on post-maturity trades if 0d breakpoint is non-zero. Proof idea: fuzz settlementFeeCbp0 values and assert fee charged post-maturity equals cbp0 * CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing take() with a loan token that charges a fee-on-transfer, cause the actual tokens received by Midnight to be less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets (the amount credited to claimableSettlementFee[loanToken]), making the settlement fee unclaimable because the contract's actual balance is insufficient to cover the recorded claimableSettlementFee? Preconditions: market uses a fee-on-transfer loan token; taker executes take() with non-zero settlement fee; claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by full buyerAssets - sellerAssets. Call sequence: deploy fee-on-transfer token -> touchMarket -> take(offer) -> claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) -> assert revert or partial transfer. Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] must not exceed actual token balance attributable to fees. Scoped impact: settlement fee unclaimable; feeClaimer cannot withdraw recorded fees. Proof idea: unit test with 1% fee-on-transfer token and assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance after take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing many small take() calls with dust-sized units at a TTM near a breakpoint, exploit the integer division rounding in the interpolation formula (feeLower * (end - TTM) + feeUpper * (TTM - start)) / (end - start) to systematically round the settlement fee to zero for each individual call, accumulating zero fees while the equivalent single large take would have charged a non-zero fee, causing settlement fee underclaim? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlement fee; taker splits a large take into many dust-unit takes; each individual fee rounds to zero due to integer division. Call sequence: touchMarket -> loop: take(offer, units=1) x N -> assert sum(claimableSettlementFee increments) < settlementFee(id, TTM) * totalUnits. Invariant tested: total settlement fee collected across N dust takes must equal the fee that would be collected in a single equivalent take. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed by up to N * (fee_per_unit - 1) wei per dust take. Proof idea: fuzz units from 1 to dust threshold and assert fee rounding cannot be exploited to zero out fees.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, by constructing an offer where offer.buy = true (maker is buyer, taker is seller) versus offer.buy = false (maker is seller, taker is buyer), cause the settlement fee to be charged to the wrong side (buyer vs seller), since the fee is always buyerAssets - sellerAssets and the payer is determined by buyerCallback/sellerCallback logic, potentially allowing the seller to receive sellerAssets without the buyer paying the full fee? Preconditions: offer.buy = false (taker is buyer); payer = buyerCallback != address(0) ? buyerCallback : msg.sender; settlement fee deducted from buyer's payment. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(offer with buy=false, takerCallback=address(0)) -> assert payer == msg.sender paid buyerAssets and receiver got sellerAssets. Invariant tested: settlement fee must always be borne by the buyer (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) regardless of offer direction. Scoped impact: settlement fee charged to wrong side if payer/receiver logic is inconsistent with fee direction. Proof idea: differential test buy=true vs buy=false offers with same units and assert fee direction is consistent.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender, by calling updatePosition() or triggering _updatePosition() via take()/withdraw() at block.timestamp == market.maturity, cause the continuous fee accrual formula (fee = postSlashPendingFee.mulDivDown(accrualEnd - lastAccrual, market.maturity - lastAccrual)) to compute accrualEnd = maturity and lastAccrual = maturity, resulting in a 0/0 division or zero fee when the lender has pending fee that should have been fully accrued, causing continuous fee underclaim? Preconditions: lender has credit > 0 and pendingFee > 0; lastAccrual < maturity; block.timestamp == market.maturity. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend offer) -> warp(market.maturity) -> updatePosition(market, lender) -> assert continuousFeeCredit increased by full pendingFee. Invariant tested: at maturity, all remaining pendingFee must be fully accrued to continuousFeeCredit. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed if maturity boundary causes zero accrual. Proof idea: unit test with lastAccrual = maturity - 1 and block.timestamp = maturity and assert fee = pendingFee.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged actor, by calling updatePosition() after market.maturity has passed (block.timestamp > market.maturity), cause the continuous fee to accrue beyond maturity because accrualEnd = min(block.timestamp, market.maturity) correctly caps at maturity, but if lastAccrual was set to a value > maturity (e.g., via a post-maturity updatePosition call that sets lastAccrual = block.timestamp > maturity), subsequent calls compute market.maturity - lastAccrual as underflow or zero, causing fee = 0 and making future continuous fee unclaimable? Preconditions: lender has credit; updatePosition called post-maturity setting lastAccrual = block.timestamp > maturity; subsequent updatePosition call. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take -> warp(maturity + 1) -> updatePosition -> warp(maturity + 2) -> updatePosition -> assert no double-accrual and no underflow. Invariant tested: lastAccrual must never exceed market.maturity after accrual; continuous fee must not accrue post-maturity. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable or double-accrued depending on lastAccrual vs maturity relationship. Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp across maturity boundary and assert lastAccrual <= maturity after each updatePosition.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender, by calling withdraw() with units = credit (full withdrawal), cause the pendingFeeDecrease calculation (pendingFee.mulDivUp(units, credit)) to round up to pendingFee + 1 when pendingFee * units is not divisible by credit, resulting in an underflow revert or a pendingFee decrease larger than the actual pendingFee, making the continuous fee unclaimable for that lender's share? Preconditions: lender has credit > 0 and pendingFee > 0; pendingFee * units % credit != 0; units == credit (full withdrawal). Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> warp(some time) -> withdraw(units = credit) -> assert pendingFeeDecrease <= pendingFee. Invariant tested: pendingFeeDecrease must never exceed position.pendingFee; mulDivUp rounding must not cause underflow. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable or accounting corruption if pendingFeeDecrease > pendingFee. Proof idea: fuzz credit and pendingFee values and assert mulDivUp(pendingFee, credit, credit) == pendingFee for full withdrawal.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged liquidator, by triggering bad debt realization during liquidate(), cause the continuousFeeCredit to be scaled down by the lossFactor update (continuousFeeCredit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - newLossFactor, type(uint128).max - oldLossFactor)) more aggressively than the actual credit loss, resulting in continuousFeeCredit being reduced below the amount that should be claimable, making continuous fees unclaimable? Preconditions: market has accumulated continuousFeeCredit > 0; liquidation realizes bad debt (badDebt > 0); lossFactor increases. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> warp(time) -> updatePosition -> liquidate(borrower with bad debt) -> claimContinuousFee -> assert claimed amount <= pre-liquidation continuousFeeCredit. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit after bad debt realization must equal the proportional share of fees attributable to surviving credit. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed or unclaimable after bad debt realization. Proof idea: fuzz badDebt/totalUnits ratio and assert continuousFeeCredit scaling is consistent with lossFactor.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, by calling liquidate() with badDebt > 0 when lossFactor == type(uint128).max (market loss factor maxed out), cause the continuousFeeCredit to be set to 0 (the else branch: continuousFeeCredit = 0) even when there is legitimate continuousFeeCredit that should remain claimable, making all accumulated continuous fees permanently unclaimable? Preconditions: marketState.lossFactor == type(uint128).max; continuousFeeCredit > 0; liquidation with badDebt > 0. Call sequence: touchMarket -> accumulate bad debt to max lossFactor -> liquidate(badDebt > 0) -> assert continuousFeeCredit == 0 -> claimContinuousFee -> assert revert or zero transfer. Invariant tested: continuousFeeCredit must not be zeroed unless the proportional scaling genuinely reduces it to zero. Scoped impact: continuous fee permanently unclaimable when lossFactor is maxed. Proof idea: unit test with lossFactor = type(uint128).max - 1 vs type(uint128).max and assert continuousFeeCredit handling.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (buy path, pendingFee accrual)] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a buy take() that increases a lender's credit, cause the continuous fee to be crystallized (pendingFee increased) using the market's current continuousFee rate, but if the feeSetter has just changed the continuousFee for the market via setMarketContinuousFee(), the new rate applies to the new credit increment while the old pendingFee is not retroactively updated, resulting in a lender paying a different continuous fee rate than expected for their existing credit? Preconditions: lender has existing credit with pendingFee accruing at old rate; feeSetter calls setMarketContinuousFee(id, newRate); taker executes take() that increases lender's credit. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> setMarketContinuousFee(id, newRate) -> take(lend more) -> updatePosition -> assert pendingFee reflects correct rate split. Invariant tested: continuous fee rate change must not retroactively alter the fee owed on pre-existing credit. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed or underclaimed depending on rate direction change. Proof idea: differential test with and without rate change and assert pendingFee accounting.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer, by calling claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) with amount > continuousFeeCredit, cause an underflow revert or allow overclaiming if the subtraction is unchecked, making the continuous fee accounting desync from actual token balances? Preconditions: feeClaimer calls claimContinuousFee with amount > marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> updatePosition -> claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit + 1, receiver) -> assert revert. Invariant tested: claimContinuousFee must revert or cap at continuousFeeCredit; token transfer must not exceed recorded credit. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed if underflow protection is absent. Proof idea: unit test claimContinuousFee with amount = continuousFeeCredit + 1 and assert revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer, by calling claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) with amount > claimableSettlementFee[token], cause an underflow or allow overclaiming of settlement fees beyond what was actually collected, draining loan token reserves that belong to lenders or borrowers? Preconditions: claimableSettlementFee[token] = X; feeClaimer calls claimSettlementFee(token, X + 1, receiver). Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(offer with fee) -> claimSettlementFee(token, claimableSettlementFee + 1, receiver) -> assert revert. Invariant tested: claimSettlementFee must not transfer more than claimableSettlementFee[token]; token balance must remain solvent. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed, draining lender/borrower reserves. Proof idea: unit test with amount = claimableSettlementFee[token] + 1 and assert revert or balance check.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing take() with TTM >= 360 days (the constant-fee region), cause the settlement fee to be computed as settlementFeeCbp6 * CBP regardless of how far above 360 days the TTM is, and if settlementFeeCbp6 was set to 0 by the feeSetter, result in zero settlement fee for all long-dated trades, causing systematic settlement fee underclaim for the entire life of the market? Preconditions: market with maturity > 360 days from now; feeSetter sets settlementFeeCbp6 = 0; taker executes take() with TTM > 360 days. Call sequence: touchMarket(maturity = now + 400 days) -> setMarketSettlementFee(id, 6, 0) -> take(offer) -> assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] unchanged. Invariant tested: settlement fee must be non-zero for long-dated markets unless explicitly set to zero by governance. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed to zero for all TTM > 360 days. Proof idea: fuzz settlementFeeCbp6 from 0 to max and assert fee collected matches expected.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing take() where the settlement fee (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) is computed as zero due to rounding (both buyerAssets and sellerAssets round to the same value at dust unit sizes), cause the claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to not increase even though a non-zero fee should have been charged, resulting in systematic settlement fee underclaim across many dust trades? Preconditions: market with small but non-zero settlement fee; taker executes take() with units = 1 (dust); buyerPrice and sellerPrice differ by less than 1 unit of loan token. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(offer, units=1) x many -> assert sum(claimableSettlementFee increments) > 0. Invariant tested: settlement fee must be non-zero for any take where the fee rate is non-zero and units > 0. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed to zero for dust trades. Proof idea: fuzz units from 1 to threshold and assert fee > 0 when settlementFee(id, TTM) > 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged actor, by calling updatePosition() multiple times in the same block (block.timestamp unchanged), cause the continuous fee to accrue zero for each subsequent call (since accrualEnd - lastAccrual = 0 after the first call sets lastAccrual = block.timestamp), but if the first call already set lastAccrual = block.timestamp and a subsequent take() in the same block adds new credit, the new credit's pendingFee is initialized without accruing the time since the original lastAccrual, causing continuous fee underclaim for the new credit? Preconditions: lender has credit; updatePosition called at T; same block: take() adds more credit; lastAccrual = T for both old and new credit. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> updatePosition(T) -> take(lend more, same block T) -> warp(T+1) -> updatePosition -> assert pendingFee reflects correct accrual for both credit tranches. Invariant tested: continuous fee must accrue correctly for credit added in the same block as updatePosition. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed for credit added in same block as position update. Proof idea: unit test same-block updatePosition + take and assert pendingFee.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (sell path, pendingFeeDecrease)] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a sell take() that decreases a lender's credit (sellerCreditDecrease > 0), cause the sellerPendingFeeDecrease to be computed incorrectly if the seller's position has not been updated (stale lastAccrual), resulting in the pendingFee being reduced by a stale proportion rather than the current accrued proportion, causing continuous fee overclaim or underclaim? Preconditions: lender (seller) has credit with stale lastAccrual (not updated for a long time); taker executes sell take() reducing seller's credit; sellerPendingFeeDecrease computed from stale pendingFee. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> warp(long time) -> take(sell, reduces seller credit) -> assert pendingFee reduction is proportional to current accrued state. Invariant tested: pendingFee reduction in sell take must reflect the post-accrual pendingFee, not the stale pre-accrual value. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed or underclaimed when sell take occurs without prior position update. Proof idea: compare pendingFee after sell take with and without prior updatePosition call.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (buy path, buyerPendingFeeIncrease)] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a buy take() that increases a lender's credit, cause the buyerPendingFeeIncrease to be computed using the market's continuousFee rate and the remaining time to maturity, but if the market's maturity is very close (TTM near zero), the pendingFee increase is near zero, effectively giving the lender free credit with no continuous fee obligation, causing continuous fee underclaim? Preconditions: market near maturity (TTM < 1 day); continuousFee > 0; taker executes buy take() increasing lender's credit. Call sequence: touchMarket -> warp(maturity - 1 hour) -> take(buy offer, increases lender credit) -> assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease > 0. Invariant tested: continuous fee must be charged proportionally to remaining TTM even for near-maturity trades. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed for near-maturity credit increases. Proof idea: fuzz TTM from 0 to 1 day and assert pendingFee increase is proportional to TTM.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / claimableSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged market creator, by creating two markets with the same loan token but different collateral/maturity parameters, cause the claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to aggregate fees from both markets into a single pool, and then the feeClaimer claiming fees from one market's activity effectively claims fees generated by the other market, causing settlement fee to be charged to the wrong market's participants? Preconditions: two markets share the same loanToken; both markets have non-zero settlement fees; feeClaimer claims claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] after trades in both markets. Call sequence: touchMarket(market1) -> touchMarket(market2) -> take(market1) -> take(market2) -> claimSettlementFee(loanToken, total, receiver) -> assert fees from both markets are correctly aggregated. Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] must correctly aggregate fees across all markets with the same loan token. Scoped impact: settlement fee accounting correct at token level but wrong at market level if per-market attribution is expected. Proof idea: unit test two markets with same loan token and assert total claimableSettlementFee equals sum of individual market fees.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee (CBP precision)] Can an unprivileged feeSetter, by setting a settlement fee that is not a multiple of CBP (1e12), cause the setMarketSettlementFee() to revert with FeeNotMultipleOfFeeCbp(), but if the fee is set to a value that rounds down when divided by CBP and stored as uint16, cause the stored fee to be systematically lower than intended, resulting in settlement fee underclaim across all trades in that market? Preconditions: feeSetter sets settlementFee to a value that loses precision when divided by CBP and stored as uint16; market created with this fee. Call sequence: setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, fee) -> settlementFeeCbps(id) -> assert stored cbp * CBP == fee. Invariant tested: settlement fee stored in CBP must round-trip exactly; precision loss must not cause systematic underclaim. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed by up to CBP - 1 per trade due to uint16 truncation. Proof idea: fuzz fee values and assert fee / CBP * CBP == fee (no precision loss).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.multicall / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by using multicall() to batch multiple take() calls in a single transaction, cause the settlement fee to be computed using the same block.timestamp for all calls (same TTM), but if the TTM is at a breakpoint boundary, the fee for all calls is computed using the same (potentially incorrect) breakpoint, amplifying any single-call fee rounding error across all batched takes? Preconditions: taker uses multicall to batch N take() calls; block.timestamp == market.maturity - breakpoint; each call computes fee independently. Call sequence: multicall([take(offer1), take(offer2), ..., take(offerN)]) at TTM == breakpoint -> assert total fee == N * single_take_fee. Invariant tested: multicall must not amplify fee rounding errors; total fee must equal sum of individual fees. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed by N * rounding_error per multicall. Proof idea: compare multicall(N takes) vs N individual takes and assert identical total fee.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged authorized operator, by calling SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) on behalf of a maker (via IMidnight.isAuthorized(maker, operator) == true), enable a root that contains offers with specific fee-manipulating parameters (e.g., tick at fee breakpoint boundary), and then immediately execute take() before the maker can revoke, causing settlement fee to be charged at a manipulated rate? Preconditions: operator is authorized by maker in Midnight; operator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) where root contains offers at fee-boundary ticks; operator immediately calls take(). Call sequence: setIsAuthorized(operator, true) -> operator: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> operator: take(offer from root) -> assert fee charged correctly. Invariant tested: SetterRatifier authorization must not allow operators to manipulate fee timing or rates on behalf of makers. Scoped impact: settlement fee charged at wrong rate if operator exploits fee boundary timing. Proof idea: unit test operator-enabled root with fee-boundary offers and assert fee is correct.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / updatePositionView (slashing + fee interaction)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, by realizing bad debt that increases lossFactor, cause the subsequent updatePositionView() to compute postSlashPendingFee incorrectly (postSlashPendingFee = pendingFee - pendingFee.mulDivUp(credit - postSlashCredit, credit)), where the mulDivUp rounding causes postSlashPendingFee to be lower than the proportional share of pendingFee that should survive the slash, resulting in continuous fee underclaim after bad debt? Preconditions: lender has credit and pendingFee; bad debt realized increasing lossFactor; updatePosition called after slash. Call sequence: touchMarket -> take(lend) -> warp -> liquidate(bad debt) -> updatePosition(lender) -> assert continuousFeeCredit increased by correct accrued fee. Invariant tested: postSlashPendingFee must be proportional to postSlashCredit; rounding must not cause systematic underclaim. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed after bad debt realization due to rounding in pendingFee slash. Proof idea: fuzz credit/pendingFee/lossFactor and assert postSlashPendingFee >= postSlashCredit * pendingFee / credit - 1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take (buy path, pendingFee initialization)] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a buy take() that creates a new lender position (credit was 0 before), cause the pendingFee to be initialized to a non-zero value based on the market's continuousFee and remaining TTM, but if the market's continuousFee was changed by feeSetter between market creation and this take(), the new lender's pendingFee is computed using the new rate while the market's continuousFeeCredit accounting expects the old rate, causing continuous fee overclaim or underclaim? Preconditions: market created with continuousFee = R1; feeSetter changes to R2; new lender takes buy offer. Call sequence: touchMarket(continuousFee=R1) -> setMarketContinuousFee(id, R2) -> take(buy, new lender) -> updatePosition(lender) -> assert continuousFeeCredit == expected. Invariant tested: continuous fee rate change must be consistently applied to new positions and continuousFeeCredit accounting. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed or underclaimed when rate changes between market creation and first lend. Proof idea: unit test rate change before first lend and assert fee accounting consistency.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee (TTM = 0 post-maturity)] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing take() exactly at block.timestamp == market.maturity (TTM = 0), cause the settlementFee() function to enter the timeToMaturity < 1 days branch (since 0 < 1 days) and compute the fee using interpolation between cbp0 and cbp1 with timeToMaturity = 0, resulting in fee = cbp0 * CBP (the 0d breakpoint), which is the same as post-maturity, but the take() itself may be on the pre-maturity path (debt increase allowed at maturity == block.timestamp), causing a fee inconsistency? Preconditions: block.timestamp == market.maturity; taker executes take() that increases debt. Call sequence: warp(market.maturity) -> take(sell offer, increases debt) -> assert CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity() revert or fee = cbp0 * CBP. Invariant tested: at block.timestamp == maturity, debt increase must be forbidden and fee must be the 0d breakpoint. Scoped impact: settlement fee mis-accounting at exact maturity boundary. Proof idea: unit test take() at block.timestamp == maturity and assert correct fee and debt behavior.
